---
title: "About Me"
toc: false
---

:::: {.columns}

::: {.column width="100%"}

## Hi, I'm Zak

I'm a research associate / sports scientist based in Eugene in the [Bowerman Sport Science Center](bssc.uoregon.edu). I work in a both a clinical and applied research capacity with athletes. In my free time I try to build tools that make data useful, and communicate findings clearly.

My work spans exploratory analysis, statistical modelling, and interactive Shiny applications. 

### Background

Originally from Spokane, WA I grew up an avid runner before attending Washington State University as a student athlete in cross country & track. I found my love for human performance through this experience. Wanting to understand performance further I attended Marquette University where I worked as a research assistant/analyst before starting at the Bowerman Sport Science Center. 

### Education

- B.A. Social Sciences
  - Emphasis in Kinesiology, Human Development & Psychology


- M.S. Sport & Exercise Analytics
  - Emphasis in data science

### Skills

| Area | Tools |
|------|-------|
| Data wrangling | dplyr, tidyr, data.table, janitor |
| Visualization | ggplot2, plotly, gt, reactable |
| Modelling | lm, glm, lme4, tidymodels |
| Apps | Shiny, shinydashboard, bslib |
| Reporting | Quarto, R Markdown, knitr |
| Version control | Git, GitHub |

### Currently

- 📍 Based in Eugene / Oregon
- 🔭 Working on translating data into actionable insight
- 📫 Reach me at [zakgkindl@gmail.com](mailto:zakgkindl@gmail.com)

:::

::::
